examples.title: Cohen-Sutherland Line Clipping
examples.description: Implementation of Cohen-Sutherland algorithm for line clipping against a rectangular window
examples.code:
ALGORITHM CohenSutherlandClip(x1, y1, x2, y2, xwmin, ywmin, xwmax, ywmax)
BEGIN
 REJECT = FALSE
 ACCEPT = FALSE
 
 WHILE NOT (REJECT OR ACCEPT) DO
 outcode1 = ComputeOutcode(x1, y1, xwmin, ywmin, xwmax, ywmax)
 outcode2 = ComputeOutcode(x2, y2, xwmin, ywmin, xwmax, ywmax)
 
 IF (outcode1 = 0 AND outcode2 = 0) THEN
 ACCEPT = TRUE
 ELSE IF (outcode1 AND outcode2) ≠ 0 THEN
 REJECT = TRUE
 ELSE
 // Partially visible - need to clip
 IF outcode1 ≠ 0 THEN
 outcodeOut = outcode1
 ELSE
 outcodeOut = outcode2
 END IF
 
 // Find intersection with boundary
 IF (outcodeOut AND 8) ≠ 0 THEN // Top
 x = x1 + (x2-x1) * (ywmax-y1)/(y2-y1)
 y = ywmax
 ELSE IF (outcodeOut AND 4) ≠ 0 THEN // Bottom
 x = x1 + (x2-x1) * (ywmin-y1)/(y2-y1)
 y = ywmin
 ELSE IF (outcodeOut AND 2) ≠ 0 THEN // Right
 y = y1 + (y2-y1) * (xwmax-x1)/(x2-x1)
 x = xwmax
 ELSE IF (outcodeOut AND 1) ≠ 0 THEN // Left
 y = y1 + (y2-y1) * (xwmin-x1)/(x2-x1)
 x = xwmin
 END IF
 
 // Replace outside point with intersection
 IF outcodeOut = outcode1 THEN
 x1 = x; y1 = y
 ELSE
 x2 = x; y2 = y
 END IF
 END IF
 END WHILE
 
 IF ACCEPT THEN
 RETURN (x1, y1, x2, y2)
 ELSE
 RETURN NULL
 END IF
END
examples.explanation: The algorithm uses outcodes to quickly determine trivial acceptance or rejection. For partially visible lines, it computes intersections with clipping boundaries and replaces the outside endpoint, repeating until the line is fully processed.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) average, O(k) worst case where k is number of subdivisions

examples.title: Liang-Barsky Line Clipping
examples.description: Parametric line clipping algorithm for rectangular windows
examples.code:
ALGORITHM LiangBarsky(x1, y1, x2, y2, xwmin, ywmin, xwmax, ywmax)
BEGIN
 dx = x2 - x1
 dy = y2 - y1
 
 p1 = -dx
 p2 = dx
 p3 = -dy
 p4 = dy
 
 q1 = x1 - xwmin
 q2 = xwmax - x1
 q3 = y1 - ywmin
 q4 = ywmax - y1
 
 u1 = 0.0
 u2 = 1.0
 
 // Check each boundary
 FOR i = 1 TO 4 DO
 IF p[i] = 0 THEN
 IF q[i] < 0 THEN
 RETURN NULL // Line is parallel and outside
 END IF
 ELSE
 r = q[i] / p[i]
 IF p[i] < 0 THEN
 u1 = MAX(u1, r) // Entering parameter
 ELSE
 u2 = MIN(u2, r) // Exiting parameter
 END IF
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 IF u1 > u2 THEN
 RETURN NULL // Line is completely outside
 ELSE
 // Calculate clipped endpoints
 xc1 = x1 + u1 * dx
 yc1 = y1 + u1 * dy
 xc2 = x1 + u2 * dx
 yc2 = y1 + u2 * dy
 RETURN (xc1, yc1, xc2, yc2)
 END IF
END
examples.explanation: The algorithm uses parametric form of line and computes entry (u1) and exit (u2) parameters. It updates these parameters based on each boundary's inequality. If final u1 ≤ u2, the line is clipped; otherwise it's rejected.

examples.title: Sutherland-Hodgman Polygon Clipping
examples.description: Clips a polygon against a single clipping boundary
examples.code:
ALGORITHM ClipPolygonAgainstBoundary(inputPolygon, boundary)
BEGIN
 outputPolygon = EMPTY
 n = number of vertices in inputPolygon
 
 FOR i = 0 TO n-1 DO
 // Current vertex and next vertex
 current = inputPolygon[i]
 next = inputPolygon[(i+1) MOD n]
 
 currentInside = IsInside(current, boundary)
 nextInside = IsInside(next, boundary)
 
 IF currentInside AND nextInside THEN
 // Both inside - add next vertex
 ADD next TO outputPolygon
 ELSE IF currentInside AND NOT nextInside THEN
 // Going outside - add intersection only
 intersection = FindIntersection(current, next, boundary)
 ADD intersection TO outputPolygon
 ELSE IF NOT currentInside AND nextInside THEN
 // Coming inside - add intersection and next vertex
 intersection = FindIntersection(current, next, boundary)
 ADD intersection TO outputPolygon
 ADD next TO outputPolygon
 ELSE
 // Both outside - add nothing
 // No vertices added
 END IF
 END FOR
 
 RETURN outputPolygon
END

ALGORITHM SutherlandHodgman(polygon, xwmin, ywmin, xwmax, ywmax)
BEGIN
 // Clip against all four boundaries sequentially
 result = ClipPolygonAgainstBoundary(polygon, LEFT_BOUNDARY)
 result = ClipPolygonAgainstBoundary(result, RIGHT_BOUNDARY)
 result = ClipPolygonAgainstBoundary(result, BOTTOM_BOUNDARY)
 result = ClipPolygonAgainstPolygon(result, TOP_BOUNDARY)
 RETURN result
END
examples.explanation: The algorithm processes each edge of the polygon against one boundary at a time. Vertices are added to output based on whether they are inside/outside relative to the boundary. Intersection points are computed where edges cross the boundary.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of vertices - processes each edge once per boundary

examples.title: ComputeOutcode Function
examples.description: Helper function to compute Cohen-Sutherland outcode for a point
examples.code:
FUNCTION ComputeOutcode(x, y, xwmin, ywmin, xwmax, ywmax)
BEGIN
 outcode = 0
 
 IF y > ywmax THEN
 outcode = outcode OR 8 // 1000 - Top
 END IF
 
 IF y < ywmin THEN
 outcode = outcode OR 4 // 0100 - Bottom
 END IF
 
 IF x > xwmax THEN
 outcode = outcode OR 2 // 0010 - Right
 END IF
 
 IF x < xwmin THEN
 outcode = outcode OR 1 // 0001 - Left
 END IF
 
 RETURN outcode
END
examples.explanation: This function assigns a 4-bit outcode to a point based on its position relative to the clipping window. Each bit represents whether the point is beyond one of the four boundaries.
